name: ESOPHAGEAL VARICES
definition: plexuses formed by (1) dilated subepithelial and submucosal veins + (2) dilated venae collateral blood flow from portal vein → azygos vein → into SVC (usually lower collateral blood flow from SVC → azygos vein → into IVC / portal venous system (upper part of intrathoracic portosystemic collateral network dilated pericardiophrenic veins mimicking a tumor
dominant_finding: dilated collateral vessels surrounding esophagus + descending thoracic aorta
discriminating_features: lobulated masses in posterior mediastinum (visible in 5-8% of patients with varices)
abnormal convex contour of azygoesophageal recess at level of gastroesophageal junction
thickened sinuous interrupted mucosal folds (earliest sign)
tortuous radiolucencies of variable size + location

general: lobulated masses in posterior mediastinum (visible in 5-8% of patients with varices)
abnormal convex contour of azygoesophageal recess at level of gastroesophageal junction
thickened sinuous interrupted mucosal folds (earliest sign)
tortuous radiolucencies of variable size + location
findings may be accentuated after sclerotherapy
nodular thickening of esophageal wall + lobulated outer contour
scalloped enhancing nodular masses protruding into esophageal lumen
right- / left-sided soft-tissue masses (= paraesophageal varices)
varices in lower ½ of esophagus
varices in upper ⅓ of esophagus
CXR: lateral bulging of paraspinal interfaces with obliteration of azygoesophageal recess +
CT: dilated collateral vessels surrounding esophagus + descending thoracic aorta

clinical.Cx: variceal hemorrhage at a rate of 10-30% per year; 20-35% mortality with exsanguination
